programme.title: To the Manor Born: Britain's Best Loved Comedy
programme.desc: Laugh-filled look at the hit sitcom starring Dame Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles, featuring a new interview with Dame Penelope, plus classic clips and archive.
A look back at the hit sitcom starring Dame Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles.

programme.title: Lip-Reading the Royals: What Are They Really Saying?
programme.desc: Britain's most skilled lip readers look beyond the headlines to reveal what they believe was being said in the extraordinary days following the death of Queen Elizabeth II. As the nation absorbs the news, cameras capture a deeply personal exchange between Princess Anne and her husband. Our lip-readers believe their words reveal not protocol, but reflection. For the royal family, grief cannot pause duty. In one poignant moment before his first televised address, Charles is seen preparing himself for history. According to our experts, he appears to say: 'I certainly must address the nation, it's my duty. I will avoid the room until then'. It's a striking glimpse of the new King balancing private sorrow with public responsibility. Then there is the walkabout that made global headlines. William, Catherine, Harry and Meghan reunited to view the sea of flowers. The images suggest unity. Yet our lip-readers detected tension beneath the surface. This episode doesn't just follow history; it reveals the human side of the royals who are battling internal grief and public duty. Series Prod: Ben Algar; Exec Prod: Jonathan Levi; Dirs: Ben Algar/Jason Davidson/Pete Roch; Content Kings (S1 Ep3)
Forensic lip readers try to decode and reveal the royals' most guarded moments.

programme.title: Queen Victoria & the Groomsman: The Scandal That Nearly Brought Down the Empire
programme.desc: Exploring a passionate love affair that nearly changed the course of British history. This programme unravels one of the most provocative royal mysteries of the 19th century - a whispered romance between the widowed Queen Victoria and her devoted Highland servant John Brown. With newly unearthed evidence and expert analysis, this shocking documentary explores claims of a secret marriage, hidden correspondence, and a potential heir that could have rocked the foundations of the British monarchy. Blending historical intrigue with emotional depth, the programme invites viewers to reconsider the private passions behind the public persona of Britain's longest-reigning queen. Series Prod: Graeme Hart; Exec Prod: Pauline Law; IWC Media

programme.title: Those Were the Days

programme.desc: Strangers from opposite sides of the wealth divide travel together on two wildly different holidays. Wealthy rock star Rob and his wife Lydia, a high-end jewellery consultant, travel together to Cyprus with hard-up best friends Shelley and Vicky to experience two wildly different holidays. Shelley and Vicky are 'here for a good time, not a long time', and love cheap getaways to the sun where they can let their hair down, party and hit the karaoke clubs. But Lydia prefers a fancy dinner and an early bedtime, so how will her and Rob get along with their holiday companions? programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: In a wonderfully heart-warming episode, millionaire parents swap with a single mum. Matt and Danni run their own luxury mattress and bedding business and have created quite a life for their family. Home is a sprawling £1.2m country house. But success has come at a cost. Work dominates Matt's life and he's often up grafting at 4am. Danni hopes living like the other half will help recalibrate their work-life balance. For Katie and her daughters home is a run-down three bed in desperate need of updating, and Katie works four jobs to make ends meet. Katie's fourth job selling pre-loved clothes is the big dream to a better life but it currently only pulls in a few pounds a week. Desperate to expand, will the swap show Katie the way ahead? Exec Prods: Emma Read/David Emerson; Dirs: Darren Green/Ricardo Hawkes; Emporium Productions (S12 Ep5)

programme.desc: The police get a call from a member of the public who has just been robbed by a gang of males armed with a crowbar and an air rifle. A pursuit goes off road and the car is lost, but with the help of the police chopper, the gang are discovered - hiding out in a hot tub! Cops also spot a BMW driver with his foot down. When the police get behind the car, the rear wheel is so worn down that sparks are coming from the tyre! And after some checks, the vehicle also has links to drugs. And in Hull, PCs race after a report of a pedestrian knocked over by a car. Series Prod: Johnny Perks; Exec Prods: Zoë Callan/Dympna Jackson; Dirs: John Nichols/Phil Hirst/Emma Fraser; Purple Productions (S9 Ep7)

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Taking Down the Shoplifting Gangs
programme.desc: After Roads Police in Beverley stopped a man linked to organised crime - finding £1300 in stolen goods and a woman tied to UK-wide shop thefts - national police intelligence unit Opal connected them to £78,000 in thefts. PC Elijah must collate intelligence from 23 police forces across the country, scanning 700 clips of CCTV and putting the allegations to the suspects in interview. Will they confess? In Lancashire, police arrest notorious shoplifter 'Michael', suspected of multiple thefts and a robbery, with only 24 hours before he must either be charged or released. And In Preston, the police's shoplifting task force Operation Vulture locate 'Ben', a well-known shoplifter suspected of stealing eight times from the same supermarket. Series Prod/Dir: David McConnell; Prods/Dirs: Josh Beattie/Cara Bowen; Exec Prods: Tom Edwards/Steve Warr; Raw Cut (S1 Ep4)
We follow the police battling the shoplifting gangs hitting high streets across the UK.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Mother and daughter farmers rush their ewe to the Huddersfield practice's farm animal centre, where Shona Searson immediately suspects a Caesarean might be needed to save the first-time mum's twin lambs. Peter Wright needs to keep his cool when dealing with the Greens' calf Frosty, who's a bit of a hot head. Meanwhile, Matt Smith and Rohin Aojula investigate the incredible 'tail' of the tiger who came for much more than just tea in their village of Holmfirth, back in the 1940s. Exec Prods: Mark McMullen/Melanie Darlaston; Senior Prod/Dir: Adam Caldow; Prods/Dirs: Jacob Hesmondhalgh/Laura Blair; Daisybeck/Group M (S22 Ep4)
Get a taste of the country life with Julian, Peter and the veterinary teams of Yorkshire.

programme.title: Ellis
programme.desc: The village of Elmsly is shaken when student Abi Marshall is found crushed beneath fallen scaffolding at Quinn Artisan Stone - a construction firm and the area's economic powerhouse. Owner Elliot Quinn calls it a tragic accident, eager to shield his business from bad press, but Ellis isn't convinced. Ellis soon sees how deep the Quinns' influence runs, and the deeper she digs, the more hints of illicit activity begin to surface at the edges of the business. In Elmsly, loyalty and fear bind people as tightly as any law, and the few facts Ellis can prise free suggest Abi came dangerously close to exposing something. (Concludes tomorrow.) Writer: Oliver Frampton; Dir: Max Myers; Prod: Martin Mahon (S2 Ep3/4)
Sharon D Clarke stars as a tenacious cop parachuted in to resolve failing investigations.
